sociology.title_zh: Networks at Work: Officer Diversity, Network Segregation, and Police Misconduct

anthropology.title_en: Islam at the monastery: on infinity as subtractive truth
anthropology.title_zh: Islam at the monastery: on infinity as subtractive truth
anthropology.title_zh_full: Islam at the monastery: on infinity as subtractive truth

meta.期刊: Journal of the Royal Anthropological Institute

anthropology.intro: Based on ethnographic research at Rūm Orthodox Christian monasteries in Lebanon, the article studies scenes of Islam at the monastery as they intersect with anxious public debates on, and anthropological theorizations of, sectarianism and ‘Muslim–Christian’ relations in the Mashriq. The article demonstrates that these monastics, when speaking about Islam's imbrication with Orthodox Christianity, defer the desire for mediation and thus effect truth as what is neither language nor body. What subsequently appears is an element permanently exempted from sociological or theological mediation – points of actual infinity. To prove this, the article utilizes two modes of thinking: the so‐called ‘apophatic’ tradition of Orthodox theology and modern mathematical set theory.
anthropology.positioning: 2026-06-03 发表于 Journal of the Royal Anthropological Institute 的新作

political_science.title_en: The Domestic Sources of International Trust
political_science.title_zh: The Domestic Sources of International Trust
political_science.title_zh_full: The Domestic Sources of International Trust

meta.作者: Michael A. Goldfien, Michael F. Joseph, Roseanne W. McManus

political_science.intro: How do states overcome mistrust? Scholars argue that costly foreign policy signals build trust. But when trust is low, such as during rivalries, states are unwilling to use these signals for fear of being cheated. We argue that domestic policies can also build trust by revealing information about a state’s likelihood of cooperating internationally when there is a correlation between domestic and international preferences. We further argue that domestic policies have a distinct advantage: the value states accrue from them depends less on international reciprocation. As a result, domestic choices can reassure counterparts at moments when trust is so low that costly international signals appear prohibitively risky. We test the implications of our theory in case studies of the Cold War’s end and United States–South Korea trust-building post-coup, illuminating several phenomena the current literature struggles to explain: initial trust-building between enduring rivals, asymmetric trust-building, and trust-building through illiberal domestic policies.
political_science.positioning: 2026-06-08 发表于 American Political Science Review 的新作
